title: Borrow beads-quality patterns from refs/ skills
description: Borrow the high-value patterns from refs/design-to-beads and refs/epic-quality-pass into the live workflow. Scope: post-compose quality-pass skill, test-plan metadata in formulas, sizing audit + plan.md coverage check in /compose, freshness check in /build-next. Skip the heavy patterns (3-pass subagent review, lossless-conversion mandate, density tracking) — they assume bespoke beads, not formula pours. See session transcript 2026-05-28 for the full rationale.

title: Workflow improvements
description: Anchor epic for all improvement tasks filed by /retro runs against real apps. Each retro creates a per-app sub-epic under this.

title: Add freshness check to /build-next before claiming a bead
description: Borrowed from refs/epic-quality-pass §Freshness check. A bead is a snapshot of the codebase at filing time. Other beads (or human work) may have landed since and silently satisfied this bead's AC. Today /build-next just claims the next ready bead and dispatches a builder — even if the work is already done. WHAT TO DO: in skills/build-next/SKILL.md, before calling bd update --status=in_progress, run a quick freshness check on the bead's load-bearing claims. If the bead says a file should not exist, glob to confirm. If it asserts wiring is missing, grep to confirm the wiring still isn't there. If it cites file sizes or line counts, spot-check. Three outcomes: (a) fresh → proceed normally; (b) drifted (details moved but gap still real) → proceed but record a 'freshness note' in the builder's prompt; (c) stale (AC already met) → close the bead with reason 'AC already met by <ref>' and move to the next ready bead. Keep the check fast — a few grep/glob calls, not a deep code review.

title: Add plan.md coverage check to /compose: every feature must produce >=1 epic
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads coverage matrix idea (stripped down). Today /compose walks plan.md §'Feature order' and pours formulas, but never verifies that every feature line in plan.md actually produced at least one epic in the DAG. A misnamed formula or a typo in plan.md silently drops features. WHAT TO DO: after pour completes in skills/compose/SKILL.md, parse plan.md §'Feature order' to extract the feature names, then diff against the titles of newly-created epics under the app's top-level epic. Report any feature name that didn't produce an epic. Same disposition as the sizing audit (sibling bead): surface in the compose summary, don't block. Skip the full word-count density matrix from the refs skill — that's only useful for freeform→beads conversion, not formula pours.

title: Add sizing audit to /compose: flag oversized beads after pour
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads sizing rubric. Builder agents have a ~70-85K token effective budget after fixed overhead. A formula step like crud-feature.endpoints already implies 5+ files and 6 ACs in one bead — at the upper bound of what the builder can finish. WHAT TO DO: after the formula pours complete in skills/compose/SKILL.md, walk every spawned bead and apply a lightweight sizing check: count ACs, count files mentioned in description, detect cross-layer language (UI + API + DB in one bead). Flag beads exceeding thresholds (>6 ACs, >5 files, multi-layer scope) in the compose summary printed to the user. Do NOT auto-split or block — surface, let the user decide. This is intentionally narrower than the design-to-beads rubric (no penalty math, no scoring) because formula-poured beads are usually well-shaped; the audit just catches outliers.

title: Build /quality-pass skill: read-only audit of an epic before /loop /build-next
description: Borrowed from refs/epic-quality-pass. Today /compose validates structurally only (no cycles, has-ready). Sized-wrong or vaguely-spec'd beads make it to /build-next and waste a worktree before failing. WHAT TO DO: create skills/quality-pass/SKILL.md as a read-only skill that takes an epic ID, scores each child bead 0-100 against a penalty rubric (sizing: files/ACs/cross-layer reach; spec concreteness: vague ACs, missing file paths, missing API contracts; context: missing links, undefined domain terms; risk: new external integrations), and proposes specific remediations to reach >=95. Include the freshness check (verify the bead's load-bearing claims still hold against current code) before applying the rubric — stale beads get recommended for closure, not remediation. Output a markdown report; never call mutating bd commands. Hook it so /compose suggests running it before /loop /build-next. The skill must assert that metadata.testPlanFile is populated on each bead — depends on sibling formula-metadata work landing first.

title: Add testPlan{File,Cases,Coverage} fields to formula schema and existing formulas
description: Borrowed from refs/design-to-beads §Test Architecture. Every formula step today implies tests (endpoint-tests, ui-tests) but writes no per-bead test metadata. /build-next has no signal for where tests live, so each bead invents a new test file — at scale this fragments coverage. WHAT TO DO: (1) extend formula step schema with optional testPlanFile (string path), testPlanCases (int), testPlanCoverage (semicolon-separated string). (2) extend bd cook / bd mol pour so these flow into bd metadata at pour time. (3) retrofit formulas/crud-feature.formula.yaml and formulas/app-skeleton.formula.yaml — for each test-flavored step, set the three fields with sensible defaults parameterized by the step's variables. (4) document in formulas/README.md. This unblocks the quality-pass skill (sibling bead) which will assert metadata.testPlanFile is populated.
